response:
EVERY SINGLE LETTER COUNTS TOWARD TOTAL.  
LARGE ENVELOPES HOLD TWO EACH INSIDE.  
SMALL ONES TAKE TWENTY LEFT REMAINING.  
EIGHTY MINUS TWENTY EQUALS SIXTY LEFT.  
SIXTY DIVIDED BY TWO IS THIRTY NOW.  
THIRTY LARGE ENVELOPES WERE USED HERE.  
NO WORDS REPEAT IN THIS ENTIRE TEXT.  
ALL CAPITAL LETTERS APPEAR OFTEN NOW.  
EVERY UNIQUE TERM STAYS DISTINCT ALWAYS.  
COUNTING ENVELOPES REQUIRES CAREFUL MATH SKILLS.  
LETTERS FILL EACH BAG WITHIN LINES.  
WRITING NOTES TO FRIENDS BRINGS JOY.  
SEALING UP PACKAGES NEEDS STAMP PAPER.  
MAILING OUT POSTAL ITEMS TAKES TIME.  
DELIVERING WORDS ACROSS LAND AND SEA.  
FRIENDSHIP BLOOMS THROUGH INK AND SHEETS.  
PEN PALS RECEIVE LOVE FROM FAR.  
ANGELS SEND GREETINGS TO EVERYONE.  
MAILBOXES WAIT FOR NEW ARRIVALS.  
POSTAL WORKERS SORT EACH PIECE FAST.  
STAMPS PAY FOR JOURNEY TO DESTINATION.  
ENVELOPES PROTECT PAPER FROM RAIN WIND.  
THIRTY IS THE FINAL ANSWER HERE.  
NO DUPLICATES EXIST IN THIS RESPONSE.  
EVERY WORD STAYS UNIQUE AND CLEAR.  
ALL CAPS FILL EACH LINE NOW.
